FULL CONTENT:
PROPOSED STUDY WEEK – POMORSKIE REGION ONE WEEK FACT-FINDING MISSION POMORSKIE POLICY REVIEW ON ENTREPRENEURSHIP AND LOCAL EMERGING INDUSTRIES Draft Time / Day Monday 11-Sep-17 Okopowa str. 21/27, Gdańsk Room 133c Tuesday 12-Sep-17 ARP (OBC) Grunwaldzka 472D, Gdańsk Room … Wednesday 13-Sep-17 ARP (OBC) Grunwaldzka 472D, Gdańsk Room … Thursday 14-Sep-17 ARP (OBC) Grunwaldzka 472D, Gdańsk Room … 09.00-10.15 Opening Meeting with the Self-government of the Pomorskie Voivodship – including introductory presentation on the region and presentation of the study week agenda:  Member of the Board of the Self-government of the Pomorskie Voivodship;  Wojciech Tyborowski, (Director of the Invest in Pomerania, Pomerania Development Agency Co.) - Presentation of the Pomorskie Region  Karolina Lipińska, Deputy Director of the Department of Economic Development, Office of the Marshal of the Pomorskie Voivodship - Presentation of Pomorskie ecosystem of innovation;  Piotr Ciechowicz (Vice President of the Board of the Pomerania Development Agency Co.)  Katarzyna Matuszak (Pomerania Development Agency Co.)  Wojciech Tyborowski (Pomerania Development Agency Co.)  Alan Aleksandrowicz (President of the InvestGDA - Gdansk Economic Development Agency Ltd) - tbc PSS 1:  Leszek Wilczyński (R&D Director of the Ship Design and Research Centre Co.)  Jacek Milewski (Member of the Board of the CRIST Co.) - tbc  Zbigniew Zienowicz (President of the Hydromega Ltd.)  Łukasz Greinke (President of the Port of Gdańsk Authority Co.) - tbc  Adam Meller (President of the Port of Gdynia Authority Co.)  Iwona Rakowska (Maritime Institute in Gdańsk)  Marek Świeczkowski (Chairman of the Logistics and Transportation Cluster North-South)  Donata Strycharczyk (Logistics and Transportation Cluster North-South)  Anna Pilachowska (Project Coordinator in the Economic Foundation / Gdynia Incubator of Entrepreneurship)  Izabela Disterheft (President of the Interizon / Pomorskie ICT Cluster)  Marcin Kowalik (Managing Partner of the Black Pearls VC) 10.15 – 11.30  Prof. Krystyna Gawlikowska-Hueckel - Presentation and roundtable discussion on the findings of the background analysis of enablers and bottlenecks of the local entrepreneurial ecosystem. Present: OECD representatives:  Jonathan Porter, Sandra Hannig, International experts:  Prof Tim Vorley, Professor of Entrepreneurship at Sheffield University, UK.  Prof Fumi Kitagawa, Lecturer on Entrepreneurship and Innovation at the University of Edinburgh.  Prof Max-Peter Menzel, Professor in economic geography at Bayreuth University, Germany. Guests:  Przemysław Herman (Department of European Social Fund, Ministry of Economic Development),  Joanna Witkowska (Director of the Voivodship Labour Office in Gdańsk)  Representatives of the Marshal of the Pomorskie Voivodship  Bogdan Ołdakowski (Member of the Board of the Regional Chamber of Commerce of Pomorskie)  Representative of the Pomorskie Employers - tbc  Henryk Stasiński (President of the Free Entrepreneurship Association) PSS 2:  Leszek Pankiewicz (President/CEO of the Solwit) - tbc  Bolesław Drapella (CEO of the AirHelp Poland, COO of the AirHelp)  Natalia Laskowska (VP of Operations of the AirHelp Poland)  Borowski Grzegorz (Co-Founder & CEO of the InfoShare Foundation) Maciej Dzierżanowski (regional smart specialisation expert) 11.30 – 12.45  prof. enriched_points.expanded_content: The Pomorskie region in Poland showcases a robust entrepreneurial ecosystem, emphasizing smart specialization and identifying enablers and bottlenecks for emerging industries, enhancing regional productivity.

The Roanoke-Blacksburg ecosystem, known for its collaborative networks, fosters innovation through strategic partnerships among educational institutions, local businesses, and government, driving sustainable economic growth.
Singapore's entrepreneurial environment is characterized by strong government support, a vibrant startup culture, and access to global markets, positioning it as a leading hub for innovation.
Key lessons from these ecosystems include the importance of collaboration, tailored support mechanisms, and fostering a culture of innovation to nurture sustainable entrepreneurial growth.
